purpose: Executable keyword and importance-type monitoring config for WaveSight AI V3.1 daily Raw / Pool / business-signal generation. It prioritizes original evidence, source diversity, vertical cases, startup funding, customer adoption and industry landing; opinion-source viewpoints are paused and excluded from business-signal generation.

policy.outside_core_track_rule: P0 core tracks are monitoring anchors, not content boundaries. Keep a daily exploratory lane for industries, workflows and weak signals outside the current core tracks.
policy.keyword_search_path_role_rule: Search paths define collection intent, not evidence authority. Every fetched result must still be classified by page type, full text, source type, event_evidence and index_only_evidence before Raw/Pool use.
policy.keyword_search_community_only_policy: HN/Reddit/X/community discussion is feedback only. It must not dominate keyword-search results. Prioritize overseas big-company events, vertical product news, startup/funding news, customer adoption, industry landing, developer ecosystem, procurement, regulation and counter-evidence.
policy.anti_big_company_bias_rule: Business signals must not be filled only with big-company product news. Every daily monitor run must actively search vertical cases, customer adoption, non-big-tech product deployments, and small / mid-size company financing. If those lanes are thin, record a coverage gap instead of padding with big-company releases.

frontstage_selected_change_target_mix.important_product_or_service: 0-4, and not more than 45% of frontstage business signals unless other lanes have no eligible original evidence

raw_lane_low_volume_backfill.rule: If any required importance lane has fewer than 3 Raw candidates, run lane-specific backfill before judging Pool health. Do not lower Pool or Core Pool evidence gates.

policy.business_signal_mix_rule: Raw and Pool can exceed these counts, but Card selection must favor original-evidence vertical cases and small / mid-size company financing before adding extra big-company product announcements. Missing lanes are a quality gap, not a reason to invent or over-summarize.

raw_entry_policy.default_strategy: AI HOT daily selected + AI HOT full 24h + source registry + keyword rules first. External multi-path search expands vertical cases, customer adoption, startup/funding news, non-big-tech product deployments and industry landing; HN/community is feedback only.
raw_entry_policy.aihot_daily_selected_pool_policy: All AI HOT daily selected items enter the Raw list and Pool index in full and receive higher capture priority. This preserves the curated daily agenda for review, but each item remains evidence-gated; only items with readable source text, usable extraction, concrete event/change evidence and non-index page type may become core_pool.

aihot_conditional_candidate_categories.paper: Paper items enter Raw Candidate only when they match technical iteration terms, business actions, developer ecosystem terms, or explicit application scenarios.

raw_entry_policy.aihot_fallback_rule: AI HOT itself is M-level discovery. Eligible items enter Raw Candidate after category / keyword / business-action screening; factual use requires original text and page-type checks.
external_search_activation: AI HOT filtered candidates are below the Raw minimum
important card candidates lack original text or event/change evidence
AI HOT origin fetch fails or only produces discovery_only / weak_signal text
raw_entry_policy.important_card_backfill_rule: Change cards, case cards, trend reports, daily observation and business brief claims must resolve original Raw evidence. B sources with original text do not require mandatory S/A backfill. C/M discovery can trigger watch, heatmap or user-feedback pools but cannot stand alone as company-fact evidence.

keyword_search_pre_gate.core_pool_rule: Generic search result must resolve to original evidence, pass Raw QC and page-type checks, and match one six-lane importance type with score >= 4.

business_signal_exclusions: social / opinion-source items without original S/A/B factual evidence
raw_entry_policy.vertical_case_and_sme_funding_rule: Before business-signal Card generation, Raw must include lane-specific searches for vertical cases and small / mid-size company financing. Use original article / announcement text; do not derive business facts from social opinions or discovery-only summaries.

important_case.label: important case
important_case.raw_goal: Customer cases, vertical deployments and workflow adoption with named company / customer / workflow evidence. Prefer original articles, customer stories, release notes and case posts over generic summaries.
important_case.core_minimum: Named actor plus real customer/industry scene or repeatable workflow evidence.

important_funding.label: important funding
important_funding.raw_goal: Small and mid-size AI company financing with company, amount or round, investor when available, and concrete product / market direction. Do not count funding maps, rankings, or investor essays as single-company funding signals.

important_technical_trend.label: important technical trend
important_technical_trend.raw_goal: Find model, benchmark, paper, open-source, infrastructure and cost-structure changes.
important_technical_trend.core_minimum: Release, benchmark, paper, repo, model capability, architecture or cost evidence.

important_product_or_service.label: important product or service
important_product_or_service.raw_goal: Product / service launches are useful only when they show a concrete customer workflow, integration, pricing / procurement path, deployment channel or measurable adoption. Big-company product news must not crowd out funding and vertical case lanes.
important_product_or_service.core_minimum: Named product/API/platform plus launch, update or commercial availability evidence.

important_vertical_solution.label: important vertical solution
important_vertical_solution.raw_goal: Vertical AI products or deployments in healthcare, legal, logistics, construction, insurance, manufacturing, retail, procurement, finance ops or similar concrete industries. Must identify the workflow or operational object.

search_provider_config.freshness_normalization_rule: Normalize valid provider date fields from Anysearch, Tavily, Exa and GDELT into published_at before freshness comparison; keep invalid dates blank.
search_provider_config.cross_provider_dedupe_rule: Merge provider hits before Raw selection by canonical URL and source-family title/date fingerprint, especially Reuters, financing-wire posts, product pages and company announcements.
